Intro: The ‘Radioactively Contaminated Land Exposure Assessment’ (RCLEA) methodology is the Environment Agency's recommended methodology for the exposure assessment of land with respect to radioactivity under Part 2A of the Environmental Protection Act 1990, including the relevant regulatory criteria.  When applied for Part 2A purposes it must be used in compliance with Statutory Guidance on the definition and identification of contaminated land (for example a 'pollutant linkage' must be established).  Please see CLR 13 and 14 for further details.
Instructions: This page contains general instructions for using RCLEA.  For more details see information on each page and refer to the accompanying user guide (CLR 13).

RCLEA was developed to support the extension of the Part 2A regime to radioactivity with respect to human health, and for use in the exposure assessment of land with respect to the relevant "radioactivity" criteria set out under Part 2A.  It can be used to assess potential long-term radiation exposure situations, and is applicable to potential "intervention" situations.  The exposure scenarios and associated models are specifically designed to reflect such situations, and therefore the methodology should not be used for the assessment of radiation doses from land in "practice" situations, e.g. land which is changing in use, or land on nuclear sites.  CLR 13 and 14 give further details.

Pages are colour coded. Green pages are general information and calculation control. Yellow pages contain input data. Blue pages contain results. At the top of each sheet is a name and description. Navigation is usually between Main and other sheets, so a 'Goto Main Sheet' button is generally available.  Some summary guidance or notes are presented on the sheets. In addition, 'tooltips' are avaliable (e.g. providing a more detailed description of a parameter) in certain places.  To locate a parameter appearing in an expression, type its name (e.g. F_UV) into the Name Box to the left of the formula box on the Excel toolbar and press Enter.

Generic assessments use RCLEA's default parameter values. Building type, land use, receptor age and receptor sex can be specified, or the application can be used to determine the 'worst' of the available options in the calculations. RSGVs are calculated using generic assessments in which the worst combination of circumstances is considered for a specified land use and a single specified radionuclide.

Scenario information is input via the 'Main' page and the radionuclide concentration(s) are specified on the 'Contamination' page. A summary of all parameter values used in the calculation is presented on the 'CalculationParams' page.

Calculated doses for each radionuclide and pathway are given on the 'Doses' page. If the 'worst' case has been selected for one or more options, the calculated total dose for all potential combinations is given on the 'AllDoses' page. Finally, the 'GuidelineValues' page summarises the Guideline Values (or, if relevant, RSGV value) that are calculated for the dose criteria.

Site specific assessments permit the user to modify many of the parameter values to better reflect the site of interest. Some basic parameter values, e.g. dose coefficients, cannot be changed, however. Doses and Guideline Values can then be assessed using the standard RCLEA models, but with the user's parameter value assumptions.

Land uses can be specified by clicking the appropriate button on the Main sheet. This takes the user to a sheet that allows the land use to be named, based on any one of the existing land uses, and the relevant age groups and exposure pathways entered. Once the land use is created, the user can specify land use specific parameters on the 'Land Use' page. Returning to the Main page allows the land use to be selected.

The 'Main' and 'Contamination' pages are used in the same way as for Generic Assessments. In other input sheets (denoted with a yellow background) new values can be specified in white boxes. Values changed from the default appear with a green background. Data on each sheet can be reset to default parameter values by clicking the 'Reset...' button.  Users are engouraged to make notes of the reasons for changing any value in the 'Comments' box.
Results are presented in the same way as for Generic Assessments.

The RCLEA application has been developed and tested for use with Microsoft(R) Excel 2000 or later. For best results view at 1024x768 resolution or higher.  The software is not supplied with any Warranty, and neither the Environment Agency nor Quintessa is liable for any consequences of its use. RCLEA and associated documentation are freely available to be downloaded from the Environment Agency contaminated land webpages at:
Main: Main calculation control page, which also presents a summary of results from the 'Doses' and 'GuidelineValues' pages.

For Generic calculations, any Calculation Selection can be set to 'worst', and the option resulting in the highest dose will be reported. For Site Specific calculations only receptor age and sex can be set to determine the 'worst' as all other selections will be known.
The ratio of the Effective Dose Criterion to the calculated Effective Dose.
Guideline value for worst-case scenario.  Note this value is not applicable to contamination with multiple radionuclides.
Contamination: Specification of radionuclides of interest, average activity concentrations in contaminated soil and the fraction of soil that is contaminated.

Enter a value directly into the "Measured" column below. It is recommended that measured values are used if possible.
The value will be calculated automatically and the result placed in the "Calculated" column below.  A default conversion coefficient of 3 Bq/m3 per Bq/kg Rn-226 will be adopted.

The value will be calculated automatically and the result placed in the "Calculated" column below.  Site-specific conversion coefficients can be calculated by supplying data on the SoilAndPlant and BuildingType pages.
In all cases, the value used in the calculation is shown in the "Used Value" column.
Amount of Rn-222 gas produced per Bq/kg Rn-226 (Bq m-3)
Short-lived radionuclides assumed to be present with the same activity as parents.
Doses: Calculated doses for the scenario conditions that result in the highest total dose. Doses are presented for each radionuclide and pathway.
Zero values are reported for pathways that are not considered in the calculation.
AllDoses: Summary of total doses calculated for all combinations of calculation options considered.
GuidelineValues: Guideline values are the radionuclide concentrations that would result in a dose criterion being reached.
The ratio of the Effective Dose Criterion to the calculated Effective Dose.
Activity (Bq per kg dry weight of soil)

Exceedance of guideline values may indicate the need for a more detailed assessment of radiation doses.  The user should check the assumptions made in the RCLEA methodology are applicable to their situation.
CalculationParams: Summary of the parameter values used in the calculation of the highest radiation dose for the assessment.
